All Thoughts
App Lock

How to Use

Speak your mind

Tap the big bubble (or the mic in the dock) and just talk — a task, an idea, a worry, anything. Stop talking and it captures itself.

Say several things in one breath and they become separate bubbles automatically.

It organizes itself

Every thought files itself into the right bubble — related things cluster together, new topics start new bubbles. You never sort anything by hand.

Disagree with where something landed? Open it and use Move.

Every bubble is a world

Tap any bubble to dive inside it. Speak in there to add thoughts inside that topic. Bubbles inside bubbles, as deep as your mind goes.

The trail at the top shows where you are — tap it or the home button to fly back out.

The four actions

Untangle splits a long messy thought into separate bubbles. Move refiles it anywhere. Edit rewords it. Release lets it go — done, gone from your galaxy for good.

Releasing is the point: this isn't a list that grows forever. Finish things and watch them leave.

Find anything

The list button in the dock opens All Thoughts — your whole galaxy as a scrollable tree, with search at the top. Tap any row to fly straight to that bubble.

Ask your galaxy

Instead of adding a thought, ask a question out loud — "what did I say about the trip?", "what should I focus on?" — and it answers from everything you've captured.

Bring your old notes

Paste a brain dump, a notes-app export, or any wall of text via the + button in All Thoughts — it gets untangled into organized bubbles automatically.

Import old notes
Paste anything — old notes, a transcript, a brain dump. It'll be split and filed automatically.
